body.conclusion: For these reasons, Ace Alliance finds the proposed "IO & Midgard Labs: L2 Scalability Initiative" Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action constitutional under Article II.6.1 and Article II.7.1 through II.7.6.
body.counterargumentDiscussion:
A reasonable counterargument arises from Article II.7.2 and concerns the proposal's prior-receipts disclosure. The proposal characterizes itself as a "deliberate co-venture between Input Output and Midgard Labs," names Midgard Labs in the title, and directs Workstream 3 specifically to the Midgard mainnet launch. Midgard Labs operates as Anastasia Labs, which is a separately incorporated Cardano team that has itself received approximately ₳2,162,096 via a prior Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action, of which public reporting indicates three of six promised milestones have been completed to date and the remainder remain in progress. The proposal's Prior Treasury Receipts section discloses only the IO and IO-affiliated allocations and does not mention the Anastasia Labs prior TWGA or the in-progress milestone status of that prior work. On the strongest reading, where a named co-venture partner has a substantive and undisclosed prior treasury relationship with active milestones, the Article II.7.2 disclosure floor is arguably not met as to the full scope of the action's economic counterparties. We acknowledge that this argument has force.

We reject that view on these facts. Article II.7.2 attaches to the "prospective recipient of the Treasury Withdrawals action," and the Legal Contract under Article II.7.5 names Input Output, not Midgard Labs, as the counterparty to Cardano Development Holdings; IO's disclosure of its and its affiliated entities' prior receipts satisfies the constitutional disclosure requirement as to the formal recipient. The Constitution does not prescribe a level of detail for the II.7.2 disclosure, and Article II.7 does not require every named partner or downstream subcontractor to appear as a co-disclosed recipient. Intersect, acting on behalf of Cardano Development Holdings as the named Administrator under Article II.7.5, will hold Input Output accountable for the agreed milestones through the CDH Legal Contract and the milestone-based PSSC disbursement framework, with the ability to pause and return funds on non-performance, and that accountability chain extends through IO to the work delivered by Midgard Labs as a downstream counterparty under the same legal instrument. Whether the omission of Midgard Labs and its prior Anastasia Labs treasury work from the disclosure section is acceptable in light of the proposal's co-venture framing, and whether the in-progress status of the prior Anastasia Labs milestones bears on the funding decision before DReps, are questions for DReps to weigh on the merits.
body.rationaleStatement:
A [PDF version][pdf-link] of this rationale is also made available.

[pdf-link]: ipfs://Qme7h1Kgt8DKGG3nuDgDjuQxXyGhwsu4dsEvkMXq1xzKUP

"IO & Midgard Labs: L2 Scalability Initiative" (gov_action1w0shrfxqwv95kk0v4cn34wylz25a2cmqkq5jpc0e2yrahhqava3qghg4q43) is a Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action and is therefore governed by both the general proposal standards in Article II, Section 6 and the additional Treasury Withdrawal standards in Article II, Section 7 of the Cardano Constitution.

This TWGA seeks to withdraw ₳10,425,871 from the Cardano Treasury (approximately $2,502,209 at $0.24 per ada) to fund a Layer 2 scalability initiative organized into three workstreams delivered as a co-venture between Input Output and Midgard Labs: an L2-agnostic data-availability strategy and prototype (₳1,895,613), production hardening of Hydra with DeFi reference implementations supporting live adopters Delta DeFi and Masumi (₳7,582,452), and the mainnet path for Midgard, Cardano's first permissionless optimistic rollup (₳947,806). Delivery runs across Q3 2026 through Q1 2027. Funds are directed into Intersect's 2025 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract (stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr), with subsequent migration to a 2026 TRSC once established, and the withdrawal is administered by Intersect on behalf of Cardano Development Holdings.

The procedural standards of Article II.6 are satisfied: the proposal anchors to an IPFS-hosted document with the on-chain blake2b-256 hash, a content-addressed form "immutable and incapable of being altered after submission," and the metadata provides the title, abstract, justification, and supporting materials required by II.6.2.

The substantive Article II.7 requirements are met as well. Under Article II.7.1, "Treasury Withdrawals actions must specify the terms of the withdrawal," and the proposal specifies the purpose (three named L2-scalability workstreams), period (Q3 2026 through Q1 2027 with per-workstream milestones), itemized costs (₳10,425,871 across the three workstreams plus a Funding Distribution breakdown by Development, Infrastructure, Security and Audits, Legal and Compliance, Engagement and Ecosystem support, Operations and Delivery, Governance, and Other), and refund circumstances (end-of-period sweep and proportional return on cancelled or reduced deliverables). The disclosure requirement of Article II.7.2 is met by a Prior Treasury Receipts table identifying ₳130,708,860 allocated and ₳78,459,777 withdrawn across IO workstreams, with each prior allocation tied to its on-chain governance action hash. The 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 is set, and this ₳10,425,871 ask does not exceed it, satisfying Article II.7.3 and Appendix I guardrail TREASURY-02a.

A 3rd Party Assurer funded as part of the withdrawal supports milestone acceptance against the deliverables detailed in the Legal Contract between Input Output and Cardano Development Holdings, satisfying the periodic independent audit and oversight allocation under Article II.7.4. Article II.7.5 is satisfied by Intersect's designation as Administrator, acting on behalf of Cardano Development Holdings, operating through the TRSC and PSSC framework with milestone-based disbursement controls and a five-member external Oversight Committee (Sundae Labs, Cardano Foundation, Dquadrant, Xerberus, NMKR). The custody requirement of Article II.7.6 is satisfied by the proposal's commitment that TRSC and PSSC instances cannot be staked with an SPO and will be delegated to the auto-abstain predefined DRep, with a community-audit dashboard, on the same Intersect TRSC pattern we have accepted as constitutional in prior actions in this family.
body.summary: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "IO & Midgard Labs: L2 Scalability Initiative" Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action constitutional. Rationales are archived at https://github.com/ace-alliance/ace-voting/
